title: [BUG] numFmt missing commas when applied
author: biggytech
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

Given a cell with value `1100` (number) I add formatting to it:
`row.getCell(1).numFmt = '_("$"* # ##0,00_);_("$"* (# ##0,00);_("$"* "-"??_);_(@_)';`
But the final format is corrupted.

Lib version: 3.10.0

## Steps To Reproduce

```
const wb = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const ws = wb.addWorksheet();
ws.columns = [
    {key: "forTest"},
];
const row= ws.addRow({
    forTest: 1100
});
row.getCell(1).numFmt = '_("$"* # ##0,00_);_("$"* (# ##0,00);_("$"* "-"??_);_(@_)';
```

I got this formatting string from custom Excel formats here:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255824-50317684-0fef-4f22-962b-8e6235dcac3e.png)

### **Expected result (the cell looks like this:)**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255906-7b66ce14-2968-4c5d-9e3d-e80226ce8101.png)

### **Actual result:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179253632-898051b2-8960-46b2-985e-52aa5e0735ed.png)

When I check format that ExcelJS applied to this cell, it seems to be missing some commas and using slashes that weren't in the original format string.
### **Expected format:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255445-94844456-d36c-4763-b276-fec271ca5458.png)

### **Actual format:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255219-d9e261e4-7df4-48ef-9b76-78c3e227eee6.png)

## The expected behaviour:

### **Expected format:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255445-94844456-d36c-4763-b276-fec271ca5458.png)

### **Expected result (the cell looks like this:)**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/96322771/179255906-7b66ce14-2968-4c5d-9e3d-e80226ce8101.png)

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):
Maybe I need to use some escape characters to get this formatting work. Maybe it's not a bug but is a undocumented behaviour?

comments.author: juansan7
comments.body:
I was facing a similar problem after a deep research It seems is not a problem of the library itself, you need specify the decimal separator in US-English format.

Something like this:
`row.getCell(1).numFmt =  '_("$"* # ##0.00_);_("$"* (# ##0.00);_("$"* "-"??_);_(@_)'`

comments.author: sebastienbarre
comments.body: @antablack but what does it display with then? If I use the dot "." as decimal separator, I see a dot, but I'd like to see a comma.

comments.author: jakubnavratil
comments.body:
Have you solved the problem of escaping spaces? `# #` -> `#\ #` ?
I just stumble on it and looking for solution. I figured that spaces is something not "language dependent"?

comments.author: jakubnavratil
comments.body: After playing with it, you need to specify thousand separator en US-English format too.

comments.author: axercode
comments.body:
to get the correct behavior use a **comma** instead of a space between characters `#`
❌wrong: `# ##0.00`
✅correctly:`#,##0.00`
